text:
It was a brisk autumn evening, so the cozy pub buzzed with warmth despite its autumn emptiness. Mary sat by the fireplace. Her book lay forgotten. A fly buzzed around her tea. She sighed, patting her stomach.

"I wish Sam would come home," she murmured.

Mary's heart ached for her husband. They'd been apart months now, fighting family differences. But there were moments when she felt how much he loved her — those rare moments when their laughter echoed warmly in their empty house.

She closed her eyes. Sam did return one night. They spent the evening in bed recounting old adventures.

"Remember our time at the castle? You convinced me we were princesses!"

"Of course! What princess wouldn't want a wild stallion?" she laughed.

Next morning, Mary cooked breakfast. There wasn’t much food, just toast and tea. She set a plate for herself and sat outside, overlooking the fields.

Out of the corner of her eye,
